characters.name: Edmond Dantès
characters.notes: Sailor, falsely imprisoned. Becomes the Count. Seeks justice through elaborate revenge.

characters.name: Mercédès
characters.notes: Edmond's fiancée. Becomes wife of Fernand. Later recognizes the Count.

characters.name: Abbé Faria
characters.notes: Political prisoner who befriends Edmond. Teaches him and reveals treasure location.

characters.name: Fernand Mondego
characters.notes: Jealous fisherman. Betrays Edmond. Becomes military officer and Count de Morcerf.

characters.name: Danglars
characters.notes: Supercargo. Writes denunciation letter. Becomes wealthy banker.

characters.name: Caderousse
characters.notes: Witness to betrayal. Drunken witness who warns the Count of his innocence.

characters.name: Villefort
characters.notes: Ambitious prosecutor. Betrays Edmond. Hides his illegitimate son. Later breaks down.

characters.name: Valentine de Villefort
characters.notes: Villefort's daughter. Innocent, kind. Target of poisoning. Loved by Maximilien.

characters.name: Maximilien Morrel
characters.notes: Son of the shipowner. Loves Valentine. Becomes officer and idealist.

characters.name: Héloïse de Villefort
characters.notes: Villefort's wife. Poisoner. Instrument of revenge through family tragedy.

characters.name: Haydée
characters.notes: Slave girl, daughter of Ali Pasha. Loves the Count. Symbol of redemption.

characters.name: Bertuccio
characters.notes: Servant with vendetta against Villefort. Helps the Count.

characters.name: Luigi Vampa
characters.notes: Bandit leader. Allies with the Count in Rome.

characters.name: Peppino
characters.notes: Vampa's lieutenant. Part of bandit schemes.

characters.name: Franz d'Épinay
characters.notes: Young nobleman. Engaged to Valentine initially. Rejects her.

locations.name: Marseille
locations.notes: Port city. Where Edmond begins his life and is betrayed.

locations.name: Château d'If
locations.notes: Island fortress prison. Where Edmond spends 14 years.

locations.name: Island of Monte Cristo
locations.notes: Remote island. Holds Ali Pasha's treasure.

locations.name: Rome
locations.notes: Where the Count encounters bandits and meets Haydée.

locations.name: Paris
locations.notes: Epicenter of revenge. Where all betrayers now live.

locations.name: Villefort's House (Auteuil)
locations.notes: Where Villefort and his family reside. Center of poisoning plot.

locations.name: Morcerf's House
locations.notes: Where Fernand/Count de Morcerf lives with Mercédès.

locations.name: Danglars' House
locations.notes: Banker's mansion. Where the Count infiltrates society.

locations.name: Opera House
locations.notes: Paris society gathering place. Where the Count is seen.

locations.name: Morrel's Office
locations.notes: Shipping house. Where young Maximilien works.

themes.name: Revenge and Justice
themes.notes: The central conflict—is the Count's revenge justified? Where is the line?

themes.name: Betrayal and Loyalty
themes.notes: Four men betray Edmond; innocent people suffer. Who deserves punishment?

themes.name: Redemption and Mercy
themes.notes: Can the Count forgive? Is mercy more powerful than revenge?

themes.name: Fate and Divine Providence
themes.notes: Is the Count an instrument of fate? Does God guide events?

themes.name: Social Class and Corruption
themes.notes: How society rewards corruption; the Count uses rank and wealth to penetrate it.

themes.name: Transformation and Identity
themes.notes: Edmond becomes the Count. Can identity be erased and rebuilt?

themes.name: Love and Sacrifice
themes.notes: Love as redemption—Haydée's love offers escape from revenge.

themes.name: Innocence and Guilt
themes.notes: Innocent people (Valentine, Maximilien) suffer for betrayers' sins.

misc.name: Denunciation Letter
misc.notes: Written by Danglars, denounces Edmond as Bonapartist conspirator.

misc.name: Ali Pasha's Treasure
misc.notes: Gold and jewels hidden on Monte Cristo island. Key to the Count's power.

misc.name: The Will
misc.notes: Abbé Faria's map and instructions to the treasure.

misc.name: The Count's Identity
misc.notes: Carefully constructed persona. How he gains entry to Parisian society.

misc.name: The Poisoned Wine
misc.notes: Method of slow revenge on Villefort's family.

misc.name: The Rope and Sack
misc.notes: Dantès' escape method from Château d'If.

scenes.title: The Betrothal Feast
scenes.summary: Edmond Dantès and Mercédès celebrate their wedding. Edmond is arrested as a Bonapartist conspirator and taken to prison.
scenes.notes: The inciting incident. Edmond's happiness is shattered by the denunciation letter.

scenes.title: The Prison Ship
scenes.summary: Edmond is transported to Château d'If. He meets soldiers and begins his prison journey.
scenes.notes: Edmond's entry into the fortress prison. His despair begins.

scenes.title: Arrival at Château d'If
scenes.summary: Edmond is thrown into cell. The governor treats him as a dangerous political prisoner.
scenes.notes: Edmond's first night in prison. Introduction to the fortress's cruelty.

scenes.title: Years Pass in Prison
scenes.summary: Edmond spends years in despair, nearly losing his mind. He questions his sanity and faith.
scenes.notes: Time passes. Edmond is reduced to animal existence.

scenes.title: The Abbé's Tunneling Sound
scenes.summary: Edmond hears digging from an adjacent cell. He begins to tunnel toward it, finding hope and purpose.
scenes.notes: Hope returns to Edmond. The beginning of his salvation.

scenes.title: Edmond Meets Abbé Faria
scenes.summary: Edmond breaks through to Faria's cell. The abbé becomes his mentor and friend.
scenes.notes: A turning point. Faria gives Edmond purpose through education and the promise of treasure.

scenes.title: Faria's Lessons and Secrets
scenes.summary: Abbé Faria teaches Edmond languages, science, and politics. He reveals the location of Ali Pasha's treasure.
scenes.notes: Edmond's transformation begins. He becomes educated and ambitious.

scenes.title: Faria's Death
scenes.summary: Abbé Faria dies. Edmond inherits his knowledge of the treasure and his dreams of freedom.
scenes.notes: Faria's death is Edmond's last connection to his past life. He must now act alone.

scenes.title: Edmond's Escape Plan
scenes.summary: Edmond places himself in the sack meant for Faria's body to escape the prison.
scenes.notes: Edmond's symbolic death and rebirth. He becomes someone new.

scenes.title: Escape from Château d'If
scenes.summary: Edmond is thrown into the sea in the sack. He escapes and swims to safety.
scenes.notes: Freedom at last. Edmond is reborn from the sea.

scenes.title: Rescue and Recovery
scenes.summary: A fisherman rescues Edmond. He recovers on a small island.
scenes.notes: Edmond begins his new life. The island becomes a refuge.

scenes.title: The Treasure Discovery
scenes.summary: Edmond reaches the Island of Monte Cristo and finds Ali Pasha's treasure buried there.
scenes.notes: Edmond's material foundation for revenge is secured. He becomes incredibly wealthy.

scenes.title: The New Identity
scenes.summary: Edmond adopts the identity of the Count of Monte Cristo. He learns the current fates of his betrayers.
scenes.notes: Edmond becomes a new man. He begins researching his betrayers and their current circumstances.

scenes.title: Meeting Luigi Vampa
scenes.summary: The Count encounters bandit Luigi Vampa in Rome. He befriends him and uses his connections.
scenes.notes: The Count builds alliances with those outside conventional society.

scenes.title: The Count Arrives in Paris
scenes.summary: The Count of Monte Cristo arrives in Paris with immense wealth and mysterious origins.
scenes.notes: The Count begins his infiltration of Parisian high society.

scenes.title: Society Welcomes the Count
scenes.summary: Paris society is fascinated by the mysterious, wealthy Count. Danglars hosts him; Villefort introduces him.
scenes.notes: The Count's wealth opens all doors. He is invited everywhere.

scenes.title: Chance Encounters with Betrayers
scenes.summary: The Count carefully arranges meetings with Fernand, Danglars, and Villefort without revealing himself.
scenes.notes: The Count studies his prey. Each betrayer has prospered through corruption.

scenes.title: Meeting Maximilien Morrel
scenes.summary: The Count meets Maximilien Morrel, son of the merchant who once showed Edmond kindness.
scenes.notes: Maximilien represents innocence and hope. The Count is drawn to him.

scenes.title: The Count and Valentine
scenes.summary: The Count becomes aware of Valentine de Villefort, Villefort's innocent daughter.
scenes.notes: Valentine is innocent but caught in her father's web. The Count recognizes her purity.

scenes.title: Caderousse Warns the Count
scenes.summary: Caderousse, now poor and drunken, encounters the Count and warns him of the betrayal from 14 years ago.
scenes.notes: Caderousse's drunken testimony confirms the Count's understanding of the betrayal.

scenes.title: The Count's First Revenge: Fernand's Exposure
scenes.summary: The Count orchestrates the revelation of Fernand Mondego's war crimes and cowardly past.
scenes.notes: Fernand's shame and loss of military honor is the first major revenge. Mercédès recognizes Edmond.

scenes.title: Mercédès Recognizes Her Lover
scenes.summary: Mercédès realizes the Count is Edmond Dantès. She keeps his secret and leaves Fernand.
scenes.notes: Mercédès represents the past. Her recognition haunts the Count.

scenes.title: Danglars' Financial Ruin
scenes.summary: The Count manipulates the stock market and uses his political connections to bankrupt Danglars.
scenes.notes: The Count uses money against the man who used money to betray him.

scenes.title: Villefort's Corruption Exposed
scenes.summary: The Count exposes Villefort's hidden illegitimate son and his complicity in crimes.
scenes.notes: Villefort's hidden past becomes his undoing.

scenes.title: Héloïse's Poisoning Campaign
scenes.summary: The Count discovers that Villefort's wife, Héloïse, has been poisoning family members for inheritance.
scenes.notes: The Count realizes that Villefort's family is destroying itself through Héloïse's actions.

scenes.title: Valentine in Danger
scenes.summary: Héloïse moves toward poisoning Valentine, the innocent, kind daughter who has done nothing wrong.
scenes.notes: Valentine's innocence becomes the Count's turning point. He must intervene.

scenes.title: The Count Saves Valentine
scenes.summary: The Count secretly administers antidotes to Valentine, saving her life from Héloïse's poison.
scenes.notes: The Count chooses mercy over complete destruction. His humanity reasserts itself.

scenes.title: Valentine and Maximilien's Love
scenes.summary: The Count orchestrates Valentine's escape to Maximilien, enabling their reunion and love.
scenes.notes: The Count redirects his power toward love rather than revenge. Hope emerges.

scenes.title: Villefort's Final Breakdown
scenes.summary: Villefort, destroyed by the loss of his family and exposure of his crimes, loses his mind.
scenes.notes: Villefort becomes a broken shell of a man. His ambition and corruption destroyed him.

scenes.title: Fernand's Suicide
scenes.summary: Fernand, stripped of his identity and honor, takes his own life in despair.
scenes.notes: The first betrayer faces the ultimate consequence. Suicide becomes his judgment.

scenes.title: Haydée Reveals Herself
scenes.summary: Haydée, the slave girl, reveals herself to be the daughter of Ali Pasha. She declares her love for the Count.
scenes.notes: Haydée offers the Count redemption through love. She is his path to healing.

scenes.title: The Count Questions His Revenge
scenes.summary: The Count reflects on the extent of his revenge and the innocent lives affected by his actions.
scenes.notes: The Count's moral awakening. He realizes the cost of his quest for justice.

scenes.title: Confrontation with Danglars
scenes.summary: The Count visits the bankrupt Danglars and offers him a way to redeem himself and recover some honor.
scenes.notes: The Count shows mercy to Danglars. Not all betrayers must be destroyed.

scenes.title: Danglars' Escape
scenes.summary: Danglars flees Paris, humiliated and broken but alive. He leaves to rebuild elsewhere.
scenes.notes: Danglars receives mercy rather than destruction. A contrast to the fates of others.

scenes.title: The Ball—Society's Last Judgment
scenes.summary: At a final grand ball, the Count is revealed as Edmond Dantès. Society reacts with shock and disbelief.
scenes.notes: The Count's revelation of identity is the climax of his revenge plot.

scenes.title: Maximilien's Gratitude
scenes.summary: Maximilien discovers that the Count saved Valentine's life and United them. He pledges eternal gratitude.
scenes.notes: Maximilien and Valentine represent the innocent who benefit from the Count's actions.

scenes.title: Caderousse's Final Warning
scenes.summary: Caderousse, dying, warns the Count that he has gone too far and must learn forgiveness.
scenes.notes: Even the drunken witness understands the moral complexity of the Count's revenge.

scenes.title: The Count's Choice
scenes.summary: The Count chooses Haydée and love over continued revenge. He accepts that justice has been served.
scenes.notes: The Count's transformation is complete. He moves from vengeance to hope.

scenes.title: The Final Escape
scenes.summary: The Count and Haydée sail away from Paris toward a new life together.
scenes.notes: Edmond Dantès is reborn again—not as a count of vengeance, but as a man capable of love.
